2605.18597.title: Latent Action Reparameterization for Efficient Agent Inference
2605.18597.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM) agents often rely on long sequences of low-level textual actions, resulting in large effective decision horizons and high inference cost. While prior work has focused on improving inference efficiency through system-level optimizations or prompt engineering, we argue that a key bottleneck lies in the representation of the action space itself. We propose Latent Action Reparameterization (LAR), a framework that learns a compact latent action space in which each latent action corresponds to a multi-step semantic behavior. By reparameterizing agent actions into latent units, LAR enables decision making over a shorter effective horizon while preserving the expressiveness of the original action space. Unlike hand-crafted macros or hierarchical controllers, latent actions are learned from agent trajectories and integrated directly into the model, allowing both planning and execution to operate over abstract action representations. Across a range of LLM-based agent benchmarks, LAR significantly reduces the effective action horizon and improves inference efficiency under fixed compute budgets. As a consequence, our approach achieves substantial reductions in action tokens and corresponding wall-clock inference time, while maintaining or improving task success rates. These results suggest that action representation learning is a critical and underexplored factor in scaling efficient LLM agent inference, complementary to advances in model architecture and hardware.
2605.18597.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）代理通常依賴於長序列的低階文本行動，導致較大的有效決策視野和高推理成本。雖然先前的研究集中在通過系統層級的優化或提示工程來提高推理效率，但我們認為一個關鍵瓶頸在於行動空間本身的表徵。我們提出潛在行動重參數化（LAR），這是一個學習緊湊潛在行動空間的框架，其中每個潛在行動對應於多步語義行為。通過將代理行動重參數化為潛在單位，LAR 能夠在保持原始行動空間表達能力的同時，在較短的有效視野上進行決策。與手工製作的宏或層次控制器不同，潛在行動是從代理軌跡中學習的，並直接整合到模型中，允許規劃和執行在抽象行動表徵上運作。在一系列基於 LLM 的代理基準測試中，LAR 顯著減少了有效行動視野並在固定計算預算下提高了推理效率。因此，我們的方法在行動標記和相應的實時計算推理時間上實現了顯著減少，同時保持或提高了任務成功率。這些結果表明，行動表徵學習是擴展高效 LLM 代理推理的一個關鍵且未被充分探索的因素，與模型架構和硬體的進步互為補充。
2605.18597.author: Wenhao Huang et.al.
2605.18597.authors: Wenhao Huang, Qingwen Zeng, Qiyue Chen, Zijie Guo, Yu Sun, Cheng Yang, Siru Ouyang, Jiri Gesi, Fang Wu, Jiayi Zhang, Huaming Chen, Bang Liu, Xiangru Tang, Chenglin Wu
